title: Get A Grip
author: Gino Wickman, Mike Paton
subline: How to get your business on track and keep it there.
customHTML:
<svg viewBox="0 0 1600 2400" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid slice" style="position:absolute;inset:0;width:100%;height:100%">
  <!-- An actual hand grip: a circle of six arrow ends pointing inward, each from a different angle, converging on a red center dot. The grip in motion. -->
  <g transform="translate(800 1100)" fill="none" stroke-width="2">
    <!-- 6 arrows pointing inward from 0°, 60°, 120°, 180°, 240°, 300° -->
    <g stroke="#cccccc">
      <!-- Right -->
      <line x1="540" y1="0" x2="180" y2="0"/>
      <polygon points="180,0 220,-20 220,20" fill="#cccccc" stroke="none"/>
      <!-- Upper right -->
      <line x1="270" y1="-468" x2="90" y2="-156"/>
      <polygon points="90,-156 100,-186 130,-176" fill="#cccccc" stroke="none"/>
      <!-- Upper left -->
      <line x1="-270" y1="-468" x2="-90" y2="-156"/>
      <polygon points="-90,-156 -100,-186 -130,-176" fill="#cccccc" stroke="none"/>
      <!-- Left -->
      <line x1="-540" y1="0" x2="-180" y2="0"/>
      <polygon points="-180,0 -220,-20 -220,20" fill="#cccccc" stroke="none"/>
      <!-- Lower left -->
      <line x1="-270" y1="468" x2="-90" y2="156"/>
      <polygon points="-90,156 -100,186 -130,176" fill="#cccccc" stroke="none"/>
      <!-- Lower right -->
      <line x1="270" y1="468" x2="90" y2="156"/>
      <polygon points="90,156 100,186 130,176" fill="#cccccc" stroke="none"/>
    </g>
    <!-- The red center — the grip point -->
    <circle cx="0" cy="0" r="40" fill="#fc1c46"/>
    <text x="0" y="6" text-anchor="middle" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="14" font-weight="500" letter-spacing="0.18em" fill="#ffffff">GRIP</text>
  </g>
</svg>
rationale: Six arrows converging on a single red dot — a literal visual pun on 'getting a grip.' 480px command-weight uppercase title 'GET A GRIP' sits at the bottom, the type doing the same visual work as the arrows. The single red is the focal point — the focus every leader is told to find.
